🧮 Conceptual Explanations
High-Level Architecture
SCBE-AETHERMOORE is a 14-layer cryptographic defense architecture combining post-quantum cryptography (PQC), geometric trust manifolds, and multi-dimensional authentication for AI governance and secure data operations.
Core Components:
14-Layer Defense Stack — Modular layers from authentication (Layer 9) through entropic defense (Layer 12) to topological CFI/PQC protocols (Layer 14)
Post-Quantum Cryptography — ML-KEM (Kyber) for key encapsulation, ML-DSA (Dilithium) for digital signatures, SHA3-256/HKDF for key derivation
Geometric Trust Manifold — Hyperbolic geometry creates exponential cost scaling for attackers: H(d, R) = R^(d²)
Six Sacred Tongues — Domain separation via linguistic primitives (KO/Intent, AV/Metadata, RU/Binding, CA/Code, UM/Secret, DR/Structure)
Concentric Rings — Radial distance from core determines security policy (PoW requirements, latency budgets, signature types)
Sacred Eggs — Ritual-based secret containers with multi-factor unsealing requirements
Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) Integration — Optional quantum-secured key exchange with BB84/E91 protocols for information-theoretic security
Anti-Fragile Design — System strengthens under attack through adaptive entropy injection and real-time threat detection
Security Properties:
PQC-resistant against quantum computers (NIST-approved algorithms)
Geometric amplification of attack costs (exponential scaling by dimensional depth)
Multi-faceted auditability with hashed telemetry for non-repudiation
Governance-first architecture with policy enforcement at each layer
Hybrid classical-quantum security for maximum resilience
What We Can Show (Without Giving Away the System)
Conceptual Architecture:
Layer Count & Purpose — 14 distinct security layers, each with specific cryptographic and governance roles
Algorithm Categories — PQC (NIST-approved), symmetric encryption, hash functions, key derivation
Design Philosophy — Geometric trust amplification, multi-dimensional authentication, anti-fragile response
Mathematical Concepts (Theory Only):
Harmonic/Hyperbolic Wall concept: H(d, R) = R^(d²) — cost scales exponentially with dimensional depth
Phase/weight relationships in cross-tokenization — linguistic tokens create verifiable trust proofs
Ring descent mechanics — security policy enforcement based on radial distance from core
What Makes It Unique:
Six Sacred Tongues — Domain-specific linguistic primitives for secure communication (names and concept only, not mappings)
Sacred Eggs — Ritual-based secret containers (conceptual framework, not unsealing logic)
Topological CFI — Control flow integrity using geometric invariants (approach described, not implementation)
Performance Claims (Simulated):
Quantum resistance: Security levels equivalent to AES-256+ against quantum attacks
Attack cost amplification: 10^6 to 10^12 computational cost multiplier depending on depth
Throughput: Suitable for enterprise AI governance and high-security data operations
What We Don't Show:
Actual cryptographic key derivation sequences
Production implementation code (Python/TypeScript internals)
Sacred Tongue byte-to-token mappings
Specific ritual requirements for Sacred Eggs
Real test results or vulnerability assessments
Layer-specific protocol handshakes
